description_long: This object is a retro-styled computer monitor with a deep, boxy housing and a prominent, slightly curved glass display. The front features a green-tinted CRT screen displaying lines of code, surrounded by a thick, matte bezel with rounded corners. Below the screen is a built-in keyboard with circular keys and a numeric pad, flanked by red toggle switches on each side. The sides of the monitor show protruding, angular modules, likely for ventilation or housing internal components, and are accented with visible screws and paneling. The rear is plain and solid, with no visible ports or branding, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The overall color scheme is dark gray with subtle metallic and plastic textures, and the construction appears robust and industrial.
description: A retro, boxy computer monitor with a green CRT screen, thick matte bezel, built-in keyboard, and red toggle switches, finished in dark gray with industrial details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT screen with green code, thick bezel, built-in keyboard, and red toggle switches.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the angular side module, visible screws, and the depth of the monitor housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid back panel with no visible ports or branding.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with the same angular module and industrial detailing.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, matte, and dark gray with rounded corners, providing a robust frame around the display area and lacking decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by an integrated, sloped base with a textured footrest and side-mounted red toggle switches, constructed from the same dark plastic as the housing.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are arranged in a grid below the screen as part of the built-in keyboard, with red toggle switches on both lower sides of the base.
